latest.title: Monkeys Los monos cambian sus hábitos para engañar
latest.script_key: los monos cambian h bitos para resolver problemas f jate en c mo usan las manos con la comida porque algunos aprenden rutinas tiles de su grupo lavar o manipular la comida de otra forma les quita la suciedad y les facilita comer el h bito se propaga porque funciona qu truco de primate quieres ver despu s now the smart hands at the start make sense

latest.title: Horses don't chew with their mouths
latest.script_key: horses chew sideways not up and down like cows watch their jaws slide side to side see how the teeth grind sideways like scissors that s because their teeth are angled to mash plants into pulp not crush grains their saliva mixes in as they chew that s why they dribble did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Sheep don't just eat grass like you think
latest.script_key: sheep don t just eat grass they bite around poisonous plants that won t hurt them that s why you see them spot cleaning patches like tiny gardeners i love this one watch how they flick their heads like they re tasting the air first would you spot the ones skipping the green

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Horses can remember familiar faces
latest.script_key: horses can remember familiar faces watch the eyes and ears because horses can learn who is familiar and who feels safe that memory changes how calmly they approach people and other horses the look is part recognition part decision which herd animal next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Horses point their ears toward what matters
latest.script_key: horses show attention with their ears watch the ears before the body moves because each ear can turn toward a sound or animal the horse is tracking that small shift shows where attention is going before the next step the ears are a radar dish not decoration would you spot it

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Capas de roca que guardan el agua de la historia
latest.script_key: las capas de roca guardan el agua del pasado f jate en las rayas primero porque cada estrato puede ser lodo arena ceniza o fondo marino acumula suficientes capas y el acantilado se convierte en un registro qu pista de la roca desciframos ahora now the rock timeline at the start makes sense

latest.title: Horses don't just graze the grass
latest.script_key: horses aren t lawnmowers that s why they re always grazing that s not just eating their guts host armies of microbes that break down grass we can t digest watch this one twice see how it keeps chewing long after it stops eating that s the microbial factory working overtime did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Giraffes aren't just tall - they're walkers
latest.script_key: they walk like racehorses front legs and back legs move together not opposite like other animals that s why their gait looks so stilted watch their right hooves hit the ground at the exact same time you ever try walking like a giraffe try it on your next sidewalk lap

latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Why dog paws explain the move in the first second
latest.script_key: dog paws carry more information than most people notice watch the first second the body pauses then the paws gives the clue in this clip the recovery angle is person holding dog the clue is not the cute part it is the timing of the movement that visible cue is the whole story so the short opens on the dog instead of stretching the setup that makes the short feel observed not copied

latest.title: lioness tail twitches when prey walks by
latest.script_key: watch her tail twitch that flicks because her inner eyelid just scanned the grass for movement that s not her cubs that s why she s already crouched when you notice you ever think predators are just lazy loungers that s what i thought too
